summary: No ship: the trusted-PR fix is coherent, but one characterization fixture omits a mandatory live-state fact and hides a destructive adopt path.

findings.title: Test-quality: the non-writer PR witness models the wrong seam
findings.body: This fixture injects an open same-repository PR but leaves the remote-branch probe empty. In production, an open `isCrossRepository:false` PR for this head necessarily occupies that repository ref. The executor therefore sees `trusted === null` plus `remoteBranch !== null`, chooses `adopt`, and leased-force-pushes over the untrusted collaborator branch before attempting `gh pr create`. For the normal main base, creation then fails because that head/base PR already exists; the human PR has been rewritten and polling remains stalled. The test proves only an impossible state, so the trust boundary prevents suppression but not mutation.
findings.recommendation: Keep the locked case unchanged, but add a realistic witness combining a non-writer same-repo PR with its matching `ls-remote` OID and require zero push/create. In the executor, distinguish this occupied untrusted ref from a true orphan and fail closed before mutation.

next_steps: Add the realistic same-repo collision witness without weakening existing tests.
Make untrusted same-repo ref occupancy fail closed, then refresh the structure-gate evidence.
